$comment: Stellar Light golden-question eval — same shape as Tyler/SDF's Raven golden set and LumenLoop's #8 audit (id / category / question / expect{ liveSource, answerRegex, forbiddenRegex }), adapted for RETRIEVAL grading. Stellar Light is the data/index layer, not the answering agent, so the regexes are matched (case-insensitive) over the TOP-K SURFACED CONTENT (title+content+url), not an agent's answer. Fields: `mode` routes to the endpoint (research|projects). `answerRegex` = patterns that must EACH surface (relevance). `forbiddenRegex` = patterns that must NOT surface — the correctness lever (mirrors Raph's #8 'criteria grade relevance, not correctness' fix): a wrong/stale fact appearing in the top-k FAILS the case. `liveSource: true` marks live-fact questions the static corpus is not meant to answer (reported N/A, not a failure). Retrieval-only knobs: `expectUrlIncludes` (a substring an expected doc URL should contain), `minTopScore` (the best vector score should clear this). Storing isn't enough; the right content has to surface and the wrong content must not.
